working_patterns: Switching from a square 6x5 grid (r=1/12) to a hexagonal/staggered 5-4-5-4-5-4 row layout increased the effective radius from ~0.0833 to ~0.0938
Using the tighter of vertical and horizontal lattice constraints (R = min(1/(2+5*sqrt3), 0.1)) to set the spacing
Adding small safety shrinkage (r = R * 0.995) and centering the pattern (y_extra, x_extra) to avoid boundary-violation failures
failing_patterns: No regressions observed this batch; however the score is stuck at 0.920592 — pure equal-radius hex layouts appear to have plateaued
Applying a 0.995 safety factor wastes ~0.5% of achievable radius sum unnecessarily if boundary math is exact
hypotheses: Hex packing beats square grid because vertical spacing sqrt(3)*R < 2R allows more rows to fit, freeing radius budget
The current configuration uses only 26 of 27 hex slots, meaning one 'gap' in a row could be exploited to shift neighboring circles and enlarge some radii

Equal-radius constraint is self-imposed by the code (np.full) — the problem likely scores sum of radii, so allowing unequal radii (e.g., corner/edge circles growing into free space) would strictly increase the score
Boundary/contact circles have unused 'slack' that could be absorbed by locally enlarging radii via a post-hoc optimization (LP or physics relaxation)
suggested_directions: Drop the equal-radius assumption: after placing hex centers, solve for per-circle radii maximizing sum(r_i) subject to |c_i-c_j| >= r_i+r_j and boundary constraints (convex in r, solvable via LP or SLSQP)
Run a physics-based relaxation: alternate (a) grow each radius to its nearest constraint, (b) nudge centers to relieve pressure, repeat
Try alternative row patterns: 5-4-5-4-5-4 (26), 4-5-4-5-4-5 (27-1), or 6-5-5-5-5 variants, and compare binding constraints
Remove the 0.995 safety factor and instead compute exact R; use a small epsilon (1e-10) only to pass validator tolerance
Exploit the single missing slot (27th position) by placing the 26 circles asymmetrically and growing corner circles into the freed space
Use scipy.optimize.minimize with sum-of-radii objective and pairwise/boundary constraints, initialized from the current hex layout
